title: Classroom Objects & Prepositions of Place
description: Things around you in a classroom, and where they are — on, in, under, next to.
summary_pt_br: Objetos da sala de aula e preposições de lugar (on, in, under, next to). O inglês usa preposições específicas que nem sempre batem com o português.

exercises.explanation: Six classroom staples. Note: 'desk' is the school desk; 'table' is the general word for any table at home or a restaurant.
exercises.hint_pt_br: 'Desk' é a mesa/carteira escolar. 'Table' é a mesa de casa, restaurante, etc.

exercises.question: The book is ___ the desk.
exercises.explanation: ON = on top of (resting on a surface). The book sits on top of the desk → 'on the desk'.
exercises.hint_pt_br: ON = em cima de algo. Sobre a mesa, sobre o chão, sobre a cama.

exercises.question: My pen is ___ my backpack.
exercises.explanation: IN = inside. The pen is inside the backpack.
exercises.hint_pt_br: IN = dentro de. Na mochila = 'in the backpack'.

exercises.question: The cat is ___ the table. (the cat is below it)
exercises.explanation: UNDER = below. Easy one-to-one with Portuguese 'embaixo de'.

exercises.question: Complete: 'Maria is sitting ___ ___ Paul.' (beside Paul)
exercises.explanation: NEXT TO = beside / ao lado de. Two words — important.
Four more you'll use weekly. 'Scissors' is always plural in English — 'the scissors are sharp', never 'the scissor is sharp'.
exercises.hint_pt_br: Atenção: 'scissors' é SEMPRE plural em inglês, mesmo quando em português dizemos 'a tesoura'.

exercises.question: Complete: 'The whiteboard is ___ ___ ___ the classroom.' (at the front)
exercises.explanation: AT THE FRONT OF / AT THE BACK OF for positions in a room.
exercises.hint_pt_br: 'At the front of' = na frente de. 'At the back of' = no fundo de.

exercises.question: A teacher asks 'Is there a clock on the wall?' The right answer if yes:
exercises.explanation: For existence ('there is/are'), the short answer is 'Yes, there is.' or 'No, there isn't.' Not 'it has' (that's Portuguese 'tem').
exercises.hint_pt_br: Cuidado: em inglês 'tem' no sentido de existir é 'there is/are', não 'have'.

turns.speaker: user
turns.target: There is a book and a pencil.

turns.speaker: user
turns.target: My notebook is in my bag.

turns.speaker: ai
turns.text: How many chairs are in the room?

turns.speaker: user
turns.target: Yes, I have a new eraser.

scenes.title: Listening story — Classroom Objects & Prepositions of Place
scenes.pt_summary: Ouça a situação com atenção. Depois, escreva em inglês o que você entendeu — resumo, detalhes, seus pensamentos. O professor vai ler e comentar.
scenes.prompt_en: Write in English what you heard. A short summary is fine. Mention who spoke, what they said, and what you think about the situation.
scenes.prompt_pt: Escreva em inglês o que você ouviu. Pode ser um resumo curto. Diga quem falou, o que disseram e o que você achou.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.en: In this story, we meet Teacher and a student. Things around you in a classroom, and where they are — on, in, under, next to. Listen carefully and think about what each person says.

paragraphs.speaker: The student
paragraphs.en: There is a book and a pencil.

paragraphs.speaker: The student
paragraphs.en: My notebook is in my bag.

paragraphs.speaker: Teacher
paragraphs.en: How many chairs are in the room?

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.en: That is the end of our short scene with Teacher. Think about what you heard and how the conversation felt.
